docs.text: A university without walls Learning belongs to everyone. FreeCampus is a global community creating rigorous, open learning materials—so curiosity, not circumstance, determines how far you can go. Explore subjects → Discover our mission ↗ FC OPEN · 2026 Learn freely Share openly 01 · Open access 02 · Academic rigor 03 · Community built Education can be both excellent and free. 100% Open access ∞ Room to learn 1 Shared campus Fields of study Follow your curiosity. Start with the fundamentals. Build real understanding. Every subject is designed to take you from first principles to confident practice. 01 </> Computing Python From your first expression to thoughtful, useful programs. ↗ 02 ∑ Foundations Mathematics Patterns, proofs, and the language behind modern science. ↗ 03 λ Inquiry Science Observe closely, reason carefully, and understand our world. ↗ 04 + Always growing More to explore See the library and help shape what comes next. → Designed for understanding More than notes. A path forward. Good learning material does more than provide answers. It builds intuition, creates space to practice, and helps you see how ideas connect. Browse the learning library → 01 Begin with an idea Clear explanations introduce the essential questions and concepts. 02 Learn by doing Purposeful examples and exercises turn knowledge into skill. 03 Make it your own Open materials invite you to adapt, improve, and share what you learn. Our shared belief “Knowledge grows when it is given away. ” No paywalls. No gatekeeping. No one-size-fits-all classroom. FreeCampus brings learners, educators, writers, and builders together to make an education worthy of everyone. Build with us ↗ Your next chapter Start anywhere. Go everywhere. Explore subjects ↗
docs.title: Home

docs.text: Education is a public good FreeCampus began with a simple conviction: access to excellent learning materials should not depend on where you live, what you earn, or whether you belong to an institution. We bring together learners, educators, subject experts, editors, designers, and developers to create thoughtful materials across disciplines. The work is open, so every lesson can be examined, discussed, adapted, translated, and made better. Our principles 01 Open by default Materials should be available without fees, artificial scarcity, or hidden conditions. 02 Rigorous and humane We value accuracy and depth, expressed with clarity and respect for the learner. 03 Built together Many perspectives make stronger resources. Constructive review is part of authorship. 04 Always improving An open course is never finished. It evolves as knowledge and its community grow. A campus without gates There are no admissions forms here. No prescribed pace. You are welcome whether you are meeting a subject for the first time, returning after years away, or helping someone else understand it more deeply. Explore the subjects →

docs.title: A campus without gates

docs.text: Help build the campus You do not need to be a professor—or even an expert—to make FreeCampus better. Clear feedback from a first-time learner can be as valuable as a new chapter from an experienced teacher. Write Explain an idea Create lessons, examples, exercises, or a complete learning path in a subject you know well. Review Make it stronger Check material for accuracy, clarity, accessibility, and the questions a learner may still have. Build Improve the platform Help with the site, learning tools, documentation, testing, or other open infrastructure. Learn Share your experience Try a lesson, note where you hesitated, and help us see the material through fresh eyes. How to begin Visit the FreeCampus organization on GitHub . Look through the open projects and choose an issue that matches your interests. Introduce yourself, ask questions, and make a small first contribution. Every contribution is reviewed collaboratively. We care about the quality of the work and the experience of the people creating it. Visit FreeCampus on GitHub ↗

docs.title: How to begin

docs.text: Choose a field of study Every FreeCampus subject starts from first principles and moves toward meaningful practice. Learn at your own pace, return whenever you need, and share what you discover. Computing · Open now Python Learn to think computationally and create useful programs with one of the world’s most approachable languages. 6 learning modules → Foundations · Open now Mathematics Develop number sense, algebraic fluency, and the habit of reasoning carefully from evidence. 5 learning modules → Inquiry · In development Natural sciences Use observation, experimentation, and models to understand the physical and living world. Community authors wanted Humanities · In development History & society Read sources critically, examine systems, and discover how the past continues to shape our lives. Community authors wanted How our learning paths work Courses are modular, practical, and designed for independent study. Each module combines a focused explanation, worked examples, exercises, and references for going deeper. Everything is published openly so it can be read, reused, translated, and improved. Help create a subject →

docs.title: How our learning paths work

docs.text: Reason with confidence Mathematics is not a collection of tricks to memorize. It is a way to describe structure, test ideas, and make careful arguments. This path emphasizes why methods work—not only how to use them. 01 Numbers & structure Build strong intuition for quantities, operations, and relationships. 02 Algebraic thinking Use symbols to express patterns and reason about the unknown. 03 Functions & change Understand how quantities depend on and vary with one another. 04 Geometry & space Explore shape, measurement, transformations, and proof. 05 Data & uncertainty Draw responsible conclusions from variation and evidence. A growing course FreeCampus materials are built in public. This learning path is being prepared by the community, and modules will be published as they pass review. Contribute to mathematics →
docs.title: Mathematics

docs.title: Reason with confidence

docs.text: Learn to speak to computers Python is a powerful first programming language because its ideas are visible and its syntax stays out of the way. This learning path begins with expressions and data, then steadily builds toward programs that solve real problems. 01 Values & expressions How programs represent information and produce results. 02 Variables & decisions Give ideas names and teach programs to choose a path. 03 Loops & collections Work effectively with sequences and repeated actions. 04 Functions Break a complex problem into clear, reusable parts. 05 Files & data Read, transform, and preserve information. 06 Build a project Bring everything together in a program of your own. A growing course FreeCampus materials are built in public. This learning path is being prepared by the community, and modules will be published as they pass review. Contribute to Python →
